title: Software testing
content:
UNIT - I

Introduction: Overview of Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC), Significance of Software Testing in SDLC, Objectives and Limitations of software testing. Difference between an Error, Fault and Failure (Software Bug), Software Testing Life Cycle (STLC) and Seven Principles of Software Testing, Role of Software Testing in Software Quality.

UNIT - II

Test Case Design: Test Cases and Test Suite, Test Case Planning and Designing, Characteristics of Good Test Case Design, Format of test case.
Testing Activities: Levels of Testing - Unit, Integration Testing and System Testing. V Model for Software Testing.

UNIT - III

Types of Software Testing: Black box testing, White Box and Gray Box Testing.


1️⃣ Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC)

SDLC ek process / framework hota hai jisme bataya jata hai ki software banane ke liye step by step kya kya kaam honge.

Simple language me:

Software banane ka proper roadmap = SDLC

Example:
Agar company ko food delivery app banana hai (jaise Zomato ya Swiggy), to randomly coding start nahi karte.

Step by step process hota hai.

SDLC basic steps

1️⃣ Requirement Analysis
Client se puchte hain kya chahiye.

Example:

  • user login

  • restaurant listing

  • payment system

  • order tracking

2️⃣ Design
System ka blueprint banate hain.

Example:

  • database design

  • UI design

  • architecture

3️⃣ Implementation (Coding)
Actual coding hoti hai.

4️⃣ Testing
Software check karte hain bugs ke liye.

5️⃣ Deployment
Software users ko release karte hain.

6️⃣ Maintenance
Bugs fix karna aur updates dena.

2️⃣ Capability Maturity Model (CMM)

CMM ek benchmark system hai jo batata hai ki company ka software development process kitna mature hai.

Simple words:

Company software kitne professional aur organized tareeke se banati hai.

CMM me 5 levels hote hain.

🔴 Level 1 — Initial (70%)

Sabse basic level.

Characteristics:

  • koi proper process nahi

  • sab kuch random coding

  • deadlines miss hoti hain

  • bugs zyada

Example:

Choti startup jaha:

"jaldi code likho aur release karo"

🟠 Level 2 — Repeatable (~15%)

Yaha company basic project management use karne lagti hai.

Key areas:

  • requirements management

  • project planning

  • quality assurance

  • configuration management

Matlab:

Projects ko same process follow karke repeat kar sakte hain.

Example:

Company har project me:

  • planning

  • testing

  • documentation karti hai

🟡 Level 3 — Defined (~10%)

Yaha complete standard process hota hai.

Company ke paas defined development rules hote hain.

Important things:

  • peer reviews

  • training programs

  • organization process definition

  • integrated software management

Example:

Har developer ko pata hota hai:

  • coding standards

  • testing rules

  • documentation rules

🔵 Level 4 — Managed (<5%)

Yaha company data aur metrics se process control karti hai.

Example:

  • defect rate measure karna

  • performance metrics

  • quality tracking

Matlab:

Software quality numbers se measure hoti hai.

🟢 Level 5 — Optimizing (<1%)

Sabse advanced level.

Company continuously improvement karti hai.

Focus:

  • defect prevention

  • process improvement

  • new technology adoption

Example:

Big tech companies jaisi Google aur Microsoft usually high maturity processes follow karti hain.

3️⃣ Waterfall Model

Waterfall ek linear SDLC model hai.

Isme development step by step hota hai.

Ek phase complete hone ke baad hi next phase start hota hai.

Flow kuch aisa hota hai:

Requirements
     ↓
Design
     ↓
Implementation
     ↓
Testing
     ↓
Deployment

Isko Waterfall isliye bolte hain kyunki process waterfall ki tarah niche flow karta hai.

Waterfall Phases

1️⃣ Requirements

Client se poora software ka requirement lete hain.

Example:

Agar online shopping app banana hai:

  • product listing

  • cart

  • payment

  • order tracking

2️⃣ Design

System ka structure design karte hain.

Example:

  • database structure

  • software architecture

  • UI layout

  • algorithms

3️⃣ Implementation

Developers coding start karte hain.

Example:

  • Java

  • Python

  • C++

  • JavaScript

4️⃣ Testing

Software test kiya jata hai.

Check karte hain:

  • bugs

  • errors

  • performance

Waterfall Strengths (Advantages)

✔ Easy to understand
✔ Easy to manage
✔ Clear milestones
✔ Documentation strong hota hai

Best for:

  • stable projects

  • government systems

  • banking systems

Waterfall Weaknesses (Disadvantages)

❌ Requirements starting me hi clear hone chahiye
❌ Changes karna difficult
❌ Customer ko product late dikhta hai
❌ Testing end me hoti hai

Example problem:

Agar project 1 saal chala aur end me client bole:

"ye feature galat hai"

to pura system change karna padega 😅

Waterfall kab use karte hain

Use when:

✔ Requirements clear hain
✔ Technology known hai
✔ Existing product ka new version bana rahe ho
✔ Product ko new platform pe port karna hai

Great 👍
Ab Part-2 me baaki SDLC models simple Hinglish + examples me samjhte hain.

Is part me cover karenge:

1️⃣ V-Shaped Model
2️⃣ Prototyping Model
3️⃣ RAD Model
4️⃣ Incremental Model

4️⃣ V-Shaped SDLC Model

V-Model = Verification + Validation Model

Ye Waterfall ka improved version hai jisme development aur testing parallel plan hoti hai.

Graphically ye V shape jaisa dikhta hai.

Requirements        → Acceptance Testing
      ↓                      ↑
High Level Design   → System Testing
      ↓                      ↑
Detailed Design     → Integration Testing
      ↓                      ↑
Coding              → Unit Testing

Left side → Development
Right side → Testing

V-Model ka simple idea

Waterfall me problem ye thi:

Testing end me hoti thi.

V-Model me:

Testing planning starting se hoti hai.

Example

Suppose company ek hospital management system bana rahi hai.

Features:

  • patient registration

  • doctor scheduling

  • reports

  • billing

To development ke saath saath testing planning bhi hoti hai.

Example:

Development PhaseTesting Phase
RequirementsAcceptance testing
Architecture designSystem testing
Detailed designIntegration testing
CodingUnit testing

V-Model Strengths

✔ Testing planning early ho jati hai
✔ Har phase testable hota hai
✔ Easy project tracking
✔ Quality better hoti hai

V-Model Weaknesses

❌ Changes handle karna difficult
❌ Iteration support nahi karta
❌ Risk analysis nahi hota

V-Model kab use karte hain

Use when:

✔ requirements clear ho
✔ system highly reliable hona chahiye

Example:

  • medical software

  • banking systems

  • safety systems

5️⃣ Structured Evolutionary Prototyping Model

Is model me pehle prototype banaya jata hai.

Prototype = software ka early demo version

Fir user usko dekh kar feedback deta hai.

Simple example

Suppose ek company food delivery app bana rahi hai jaisa Zomato.

Developer directly full system nahi banata.

Pehle basic prototype banata hai:

  • login page

  • restaurant list

  • simple order button

User dekh ke bolta hai:

👉 "UI improve karo"
👉 "search option add karo"

Developers fir prototype improve karte hain.

Ye process repeat hota rehta hai.

Steps of Prototyping

1️⃣ Preliminary project plan
2️⃣ Basic design model
3️⃣ Prototype build
4️⃣ User evaluation
5️⃣ Improvements
6️⃣ Repeat until satisfied

Strengths

✔ User ko system early dikh jata hai
✔ Requirements clear ho jati hain
✔ Flexible development
✔ Customer feedback milta rehta hai

Weaknesses

❌ Developers shortcut coding kar sakte hain
❌ Maintainability issue
❌ Scope creep ho sakta hai

Scope creep =
project continuously grow karta rehta hai

Kab use karte hain

Use when:

✔ requirements unclear ho
✔ UI heavy system ho
✔ new product development ho

6️⃣ Rapid Application Development (RAD)

RAD = Rapid Application Development

Iska main goal:

⚡ Software bahut jaldi develop karna

Isme automation tools aur reusable components use karte hain.

RAD phases

1️⃣ Requirements Planning

Client aur developers meeting karte hain.

Business problems discuss hote hain.

2️⃣ User Design Phase

User system design me actively participate karta hai.

Tools use hote hain:

  • screen generators

  • UI builders

3️⃣ Construction Phase

Actual development hoti hai.

Tools use hote hain:

  • code generators

  • automation tools

4️⃣ Cutover Phase

Final steps:

  • testing

  • installation

  • user training

Example

Suppose ek startup event booking app banana chahta hai.

RAD use karke:

  • ready templates

  • UI builders

  • code generators

use karke system fast develop kar sakte hain.

RAD Strengths

✔ Fast development
✔ Customer involvement
✔ Lower cost
✔ Quick delivery

RAD Weaknesses

❌ Large projects me difficult
❌ Skilled team chahiye
❌ Legacy systems integrate karna tough

RAD kab use karte hain

Use when:

✔ requirements known ho
✔ project time-boxed ho
✔ system modular ho

7️⃣ Incremental SDLC Model

Incremental model me software parts me develop hota hai.

System ek saath nahi banta.

Instead:

multiple releases me banta hai.

Simple Example

Suppose ek company e-commerce website bana rahi hai jaisi Amazon.

Release 1

Basic features:

  • user login

  • product list

Release 2

Add:

  • cart

  • checkout

Release 3

Add:

  • payment system

  • order tracking

Final system gradually complete ho jata hai.

Incremental Model Strengths

✔ early working product milta hai
✔ important features first develop hote hain
✔ risk kam ho jata hai
✔ customers feedback de sakte hain

Incremental Model Weaknesses

❌ good planning required
❌ architecture strong hona chahiye
❌ overall cost kam nahi hota

Kab use karte hain

Use when:

✔ large projects ho
✔ requirements evolve ho sakti hain
✔ market me early release chahiye

🔑 Short Revision (Exam ke liye)

ModelMain Idea
WaterfallSequential development
V-ModelDevelopment + Testing parallel
PrototypingPrototype → feedback → improve
RADVery fast development
IncrementalSoftware parts me release


1️⃣ Spiral Model
2️⃣ Agile SDLC
3️⃣ Extreme Programming (XP)
4️⃣ Feature Driven Development (FDD)
5️⃣ Dynamic Systems Development Method (DSDM)
6️⃣ Adaptive SDLC
7️⃣ Tailored SDLC Models
8️⃣ Software Quality
9️⃣ Quality Assurance Plan

1️⃣ Spiral SDLC Model

Spiral Model = Waterfall + Risk Analysis + Prototyping

Isme development spiral loops (cycles) me hota hai.

Har cycle me:

  • planning

  • risk analysis

  • development

  • testing

hota hai.

Isko Spiral isliye bolte hain kyunki process spiral shape me repeat hota rehta hai.

Spiral Model ke 4 Quadrants

Har spiral cycle me 4 steps hote hain.

1️⃣ Determine Objectives

Is step me project goals decide hote hain.

Example:

  • functionality kya hogi

  • performance kya hogi

  • hardware/software interface

  • success factors

Also decide:

Alternatives

Example:

  • khud build karein

  • existing software reuse karein

  • third party se buy karein

Constraints

Example:

  • budget

  • schedule

  • technology limitations

2️⃣ Evaluate Alternatives and Identify Risks

Is stage me risk analysis hota hai.

Risk example:

  • new technology use karna

  • developers ko experience nahi

  • tight deadline

Fir risk solve karne ki strategy banate hain.

Example:

Prototype banake test karna.

3️⃣ Develop Next-Level Product

Ab actual development hota hai.

Typical activities:

  • design create karna

  • code likhna

  • code review karna

  • testing karna

4️⃣ Plan Next Phase

Next cycle ke liye planning hoti hai.

Example:

  • project plan

  • testing plan

  • installation plan

  • configuration management

Fir next spiral cycle start ho jata hai.

Spiral Model Advantages

✔ High-risk projects ke liye best
✔ Early prototype mil jata hai
✔ user feedback early milta hai
✔ design perfect hona zaroori nahi starting me
✔ risk early detect ho jata hai

Spiral Model Disadvantages

❌ complex model hai
❌ risk analysis expert chahiye
❌ small projects ke liye costly
❌ project kabhi kabhi infinite loop me chal sakta hai

Spiral Model kab use karte hain

Use when:

✔ high risk project ho
✔ requirements complex ho
✔ new technology use ho
✔ users unsure ho

2️⃣ Agile SDLC

Agile = Fast, flexible development approach

Traditional models (Waterfall) me problem:

  • slow development

  • changes difficult

Agile me:

⚡ software small iterations me develop hota hai.

Har iteration me:

  • development

  • testing

  • feedback

hota hai.

Example:

Apps like Instagram aur WhatsApp regularly new updates release karte hain — ye Agile approach ka example hai.

Agile Characteristics

✔ short development cycles
✔ customer involvement
✔ frequent releases
✔ flexible requirements

3️⃣ Some Agile Methods

Agile ke under bahut frameworks aate hain:

  • Adaptive Software Development (ASD)

  • Feature Driven Development (FDD)

  • Crystal Clear

  • Dynamic Software Development Method (DSDM)

  • Rapid Application Development (RAD)

  • Scrum

  • Extreme Programming (XP)

  • Rational Unified Process (RUP)

4️⃣ Extreme Programming (XP)

XP ek Agile methodology hai jo small teams ke liye best hoti hai.

Use when:

  • requirements unclear ho

  • requirements frequently change ho

XP me coding central activity hoti hai.

Communication mostly code aur tests ke through hota hai.

XP Practices

XP me 12 main practices hoti hain.

1️⃣ Planning Game

Developers aur customers milke decide karte hain:

  • next release me kya features honge

2️⃣ Small Releases

Software ko frequent small versions me release karte hain.

3️⃣ Metaphor

System ko simple story ke through explain karte hain.

Example:

System ko ek library system jaisa treat karna.

4️⃣ Simple Design

System ko jitna simple ho sake utna simple design karte hain.

5️⃣ Testing

Developers continuously unit tests likhte hain.

Customers bhi feature tests likhte hain.

6️⃣ Refactoring

Code ko improve karna without changing functionality.

Example:

duplicate code remove karna.

7️⃣ Pair Programming

Ek computer par 2 programmers kaam karte hain.

Ek code likhta hai
dusra review karta hai.

8️⃣ Collective Ownership

Project ka code koi bhi developer modify kar sakta hai.

9️⃣ Continuous Integration

Developers code ko day me multiple times integrate karte hain.

🔟 40-hour week

Developers ko overwork nahi karna chahiye.

Normal schedule maintain karte hain.

1️⃣1️⃣ On-Site Customer

Customer team ke saath hi available hota hai.

Questions instantly solve ho jate hain.

1️⃣2️⃣ Coding Standards

Team common coding rules follow karti hai.

XP ko "Extreme" kyun bolte hain?

Kyuki normal practices ko extreme level par follow karte hain.

Example:

  • testing good hai → har time testing

  • code review good hai → pair programming always

  • integration important hai → daily integration

5️⃣ Feature Driven Development (FDD)

FDD me software features ke basis par develop hota hai.

Feature example:

  • calculate order total

  • generate invoice

  • add product to cart

FDD Steps

1️⃣ Develop Overall Model

System ka overall architecture design karte hain.

2️⃣ Build Feature List

Saare features list karte hain.

Example:

  • login

  • payment

  • cart

3️⃣ Plan by Feature

Features ko development order me arrange karte hain.

4️⃣ Design by Feature

Specific feature ka design banate hain.

5️⃣ Build by Feature

Code likhte hain aur test karte hain.

6️⃣ Dynamic Systems Development Method (DSDM)

DSDM ek Agile + RAD framework hai.

Ye 80/20 rule follow karta hai.

Meaning:

80% important requirements short time me deliver ho jati hain.

DSDM Principles

✔ active user involvement
✔ frequent product delivery
✔ iterative development
✔ continuous testing
✔ team collaboration

DSDM Lifecycle

Steps:

1️⃣ feasibility study
2️⃣ business study
3️⃣ functional model iteration
4️⃣ design & build iteration
5️⃣ implementation

7️⃣ Adaptive SDLC

Adaptive model RAD + software engineering practices combine karta hai.

Steps:

1️⃣ Project initiation
2️⃣ cycle planning
3️⃣ concurrent development
4️⃣ quality review
5️⃣ release

Adaptive Steps

  • project goal define karna

  • time-box decide karna

  • cycles define karna

  • objectives assign karna

  • task list create karna

  • cycle review karna

8️⃣ Tailored SDLC Models

Reality me ek single SDLC model har project ke liye perfect nahi hota.

Isliye companies models combine kar leti hain.

Example:

  • Spiral + Incremental

  • Incremental + V-model

Example real companies like Amazon ya Google apne custom SDLC processes use karti hain.

9️⃣ Software Quality

Software quality ka meaning:

Software user requirements satisfy kare aur properly work kare.

Quality indicators:

✔ system crash na kare
✔ performance achhi ho
✔ user expectations meet kare

Poor quality software:

  • maintenance cost increase karta hai

  • business damage kar sakta hai

🔟 Quality Assurance Plan (QA Plan)

QA plan ek written plan hota hai jo batata hai:

Software quality kaise maintain hogi.

QA Plan Activities

1️⃣ Defect Tracking

Har bug record hota hai:

  • bug source

  • kab detect hua

  • kaise fix hua

2️⃣ Unit Testing

Har module individually test hota hai.

3️⃣ Source Code Tracing

Code ko line by line review karte hain.

4️⃣ Technical Reviews

Developers ek dusre ka work review karte hain.

5️⃣ Integration Testing

Different modules ko combine karke test karte hain.

6️⃣ System Testing

Complete system test hota hai.

🔑 Final Quick Summary

ConceptIdea
Spiral ModelRisk analysis + iterative development
AgileFast flexible development
XPExtreme agile practices
FDDDevelopment by features
DSDMAgile + RAD framework
AdaptiveRAD + engineering practices
Tailored ModelsCustom combination of models
Software QualityRequirements satisfaction
QA PlanQuality control activities

1️⃣ Software Testing kya hota hai?

Software Testing ka matlab hai software ko check karna ki wo sahi kaam kar raha hai ya nahi aur usme bugs (errors) to nahi hain.

Simple example:

Jab ek company app banati hai jaise WhatsApp ya Instagram, to release karne se pehle developers usko test karte hain.

Example check karte hain:

  • message send ho raha hai ya nahi

  • login kaam kar raha hai ya nahi

  • app crash to nahi ho raha

👉 Ye sab software testing hai.

2️⃣ Software Testing kyun karte hain? (Purpose)

Software testing ka main goal hai:

✔ bugs jaldi pakadna
✔ quality improve karna
✔ user ko reliable software dena

Analogy

Socho car manufacturing ho rahi hai.

Agar car bina testing ke market me bhej di:

  • brakes fail ho sakte hain

  • engine problem ho sakti hai

Isliye car ko road test kiya jata hai.

Software testing bhi software ka road test hai 🚗.

3️⃣ Key Goals of Software Testing

1️⃣ Defect Detection (Bugs dhundna)

Testing ka sabse pehla kaam hai bugs detect karna.

Example:

Agar login page me bug ho:

  • password correct hai

  • fir bhi login nahi ho raha

To testing me ye problem pakad li jati hai.

Analogy

Jaise exam se pehle practice test dena.

Practice test se pata chal jata hai:

  • kaha mistake ho rahi hai.

2️⃣ Quality Assurance (Quality ensure karna)

Testing ensure karta hai ki software:

  • stable ho

  • reliable ho

  • properly kaam kare

Example:

Agar payment app crash ho jaye to users trust lose kar dete hain.

Analogy

Restaurant me food serve karne se pehle chef taste karta hai.

Taaki customer ko bad taste na mile 🍲.

3️⃣ Requirement Validation

Testing verify karta hai ki software requirements ke according bana hai ya nahi.

Example:

Client ne bola:

  • login system chahiye

  • order tracking chahiye

Testing check karta hai ki ye features properly work kar rahe hain.

Analogy

Jaise tailor ko shirt banane ke liye measurements diye.

Agar shirt measurement ke according bani hai to requirement satisfy hui.

4️⃣ Risk Mitigation (Risk kam karna)

Testing future problems ko reduce karta hai.

Possible risks:

  • security issues

  • performance issues

  • system crash

Example:

Agar banking app hack ho jaye to huge loss ho sakta hai.

Analogy

Jaise building banane se pehle structural testing hoti hai.

Taaki building gir na jaye 🏢.

5️⃣ User Satisfaction

Testing ensure karta hai ki software:

  • easy to use ho

  • fast ho

  • bugs free ho

Example:

Agar app slow ho to users uninstall kar dete hain.

Analogy

Jaise hotel room clean na ho to customer unhappy ho jata hai.

6️⃣ Cost Reduction

Agar bug early stage me mil jaye to fix karna easy aur cheap hota hai.

Late stage me fix karna:

  • costly

  • time consuming

Example:

Coding phase me bug fix = easy
Production me bug fix = difficult

Analogy

Jaise ghar banate time foundation me problem mil jaye.

Early fix karna easy hai.

Agar building complete hone ke baad problem mile to bahut expensive ho jata hai.

7️⃣ Confidence Building

Testing stakeholders ko confidence deta hai ki software release ke liye ready hai.

Stakeholders:

  • clients

  • managers

  • developers

Analogy

Jaise exam se pehle mock test dekar student confident feel karta hai.

4️⃣ Importance of Testing in SDLC

Testing SDLC ka critical phase hai.

Early Defect Detection

Testing early stage me bugs detect karta hai.

Analogy

Jaise doctor early stage me disease detect kar le to treatment easy hota hai.

Quality Assurance

Testing ensure karta hai:

  • software correctly build hua hai

  • user needs fulfill ho rahi hain

Isko do terms me bolte hain:

Verification → software correct build hua hai
Validation → right product banaya hai

Analogy

Verification = recipe follow ki
Validation = food tasty bana ya nahi

Risk Mitigation

Testing potential problems reduce karta hai:

  • security issues

  • system crash

  • performance issues

Analogy

Jaise airplane takeoff se pehle full safety check hota hai ✈️.

Improved User Satisfaction

Testing ensure karta hai:

  • software smooth chale

  • UI friendly ho

  • bugs na ho

Analogy

Jaise phone kharidte waqt agar phone hang kare to customer unhappy ho jata hai.

Compliance and Standards

Some industries me strict rules hote hain.

Example industries:

  • medical software

  • banking systems

Testing ensure karta hai software regulations follow kare.

Analogy

Jaise car ko road pe chalane ke liye safety standards pass karne padte hain.

Informed Decision Making

Testing se developers ko feedback milta hai.

Example:

  • performance issues

  • bugs

  • design flaws

Is feedback se future software improve hota hai.

Analogy

Jaise teacher feedback deta hai assignment me.

Building Confidence

Testing stakeholders ko trust deta hai ki software real world me properly work karega.

Example:

Apps like Google Pay ya Paytm ko millions log use karte hain — isliye testing extremely important hoti hai.

5️⃣ Limitations of Software Testing

Testing important hai lekin perfect solution nahi hai.

1️⃣ Exhaustive Testing Impossible

Har possible input test karna impossible hai.

Example:

Agar form me 1000 combinations ho to sab test karna practical nahi.

Analogy

Jaise chess game me har possible move predict karna impossible.

2️⃣ Cannot Prove Absence of Bugs

Testing bugs find kar sakta hai.

Lekin guarantee nahi de sakta ki 0 bugs hain.

Analogy

Jaise police search kare aur kuch criminals mil jayein.

Par iska matlab nahi ki sab criminals pakad liye gaye.

3️⃣ Time and Resource Constraints

Testing time-consuming hota hai.

Projects me deadlines hoti hain.

Isliye kabhi kabhi testing limited time me karni padti hai.

Analogy

Jaise exam ke liye time kam ho to saare topics revise nahi kar paate.

4️⃣ Dependence on Test Data

Testing ke liye good test data chahiye.

Agar test data wrong ho to results misleading ho sakte hain.

Analogy

Jaise calculator me wrong numbers daalne se wrong result aayega.

5️⃣ Human Error

Testers bhi human hote hain.

Mistakes ho sakti hain:

  • wrong test case

  • bug miss karna

Analogy

Jaise teacher bhi kabhi exam checking me mistake kar sakta hai.

6️⃣ Changing Requirements

Agar requirements change hoti rahein to testing difficult ho jata hai.

Example:

Client bar bar new features add kar raha ho.

Analogy

Jaise building design bar bar change kar diya jaye.

7️⃣ Environment Differences

Testing environment aur real environment different ho sakte hain.

Example:

Software testing me perfect kaam karta hai
Production me crash ho jata hai.

Analogy

Jaise practice match aur real match me difference hota hai.

8️⃣ Automation Limitations

Automation tools helpful hain but perfect nahi.

Problems:

  • maintenance required

  • false alerts

  • human thinking replace nahi kar sakte

Analogy

Jaise calculator fast hai but kabhi kabhi human logic bhi chahiye.

🔑 Super Short Revision (Exam Ready)

Software testing ka purpose:

1️⃣ bugs detect karna
2️⃣ software quality ensure karna
3️⃣ requirements validate karna
4️⃣ risks reduce karna
5️⃣ user satisfaction improve karna
6️⃣ cost reduce karna
7️⃣ stakeholder confidence build karna

Limitations:

  • exhaustive testing impossible

  • bugs absence prove nahi kar sakte

  • time constraints

  • human errors


1️⃣ Error
2️⃣ Fault
3️⃣ Defect
4️⃣ Bug
5️⃣ Failure

Sab ek chain me connected hote hain.

Sequence usually hota hai:

👉 Error → Fault → Defect → Bug → Failure

Matlab ek small mistake gradually badi problem ban sakti hai.

1️⃣ What is an Error?

Error = Human mistake

Jab developer ya tester requirement galat samajh leta hai ya coding me mistake kar deta hai to usko Error bolte hain.

Ye mostly development phase me hota hai.

Example:

Developer ko bola gaya:

Bonus = salary × 20%

Developer ne likh diya:

bonus = salary * 2

Ye human mistake hai.

Isliye ye Error hai.

Analogy

Socho maths exam me formula galat yaad ho gaya.

Jaise:

Area of circle = πr²
par student likh de 2πr

Ye Error hai.

2️⃣ What is a Fault?

Fault = Error ki wajah se code me aayi problem

Jab error code me aa jata hai aur program ke logic ko damage karta hai to usko Fault bolte hain.

Ye code ke andar hidden problem hoti hai.

Example:

Developer ne galat formula code me likh diya.

Program compile ho gaya.

Lekin code ke andar logic wrong hai.

Ye Fault hai.

Analogy

Socho building ka design galat ban gaya.

Abhi building khadi hai, gir nahi rahi.

Lekin design me fault hai jo future me problem bana sakta hai.

3️⃣ What is a Defect?

Defect = Software expected requirement ke according kaam nahi kar raha

Matlab:

Expected result ≠ Actual result

Example:

Requirement:

User correct username aur password dale to login hona chahiye.

Actual:

Correct password dalne par bhi login nahi ho raha.

Ye Defect hai.

Analogy

Socho aapne online pizza order kiya 🍕

Expected: Veg pizza
Actual: Chicken pizza deliver ho gaya.

Expected aur actual match nahi kar rahe → Defect.

4️⃣ What is a Bug?

Bug = Defect jo tester detect karta hai aur report karta hai

Tester jab defect ko identify karta hai aur bug report me likhta hai, tab usko Bug bolte hain.

Example:

Tester testing kar raha hai:

Login feature test karta hai.

Login fail ho raha hai.

Tester bug report me likhta hai:

"Login feature not working with valid credentials."

Ab ye Bug hai.

Analogy

Socho car me problem hai.

Driver mechanic ko bolta hai:

Brake properly kaam nahi kar raha.

Driver ne issue report kiya → Bug report.

5️⃣ What is a Failure?

Failure = Jab software real use me kaam karna band kar de

Failure mostly production environment me hota hai.

Matlab user software use kar raha hai aur system:

  • crash ho jata hai

  • wrong output deta hai

  • hang ho jata hai

Example:

User payment kar raha hai.

App suddenly crash ho gaya.

Ye Failure hai.

Example apps like Google Pay ya Paytm agar payment ke time crash ho jaye to wo software failure hai.

Analogy

Socho car drive karte waqt engine band ho jaye.

Ye Failure hai.

Simple Chain (Very Important)

Sab terms ka connection:

Error → Fault → Defect → Bug → Failure

Example:

Developer formula galat likhta hai
→ Error

Code me wrong logic aa jata hai
→ Fault

Software wrong result deta hai
→ Defect

Tester issue report karta hai
→ Bug

User use karte time system crash ho jata hai
→ Failure

Examples of Bugs

1️⃣ Syntax Bug

Programming rule break ho gaya.

Example:

semicolon missing

printf("Hello")

Should be

printf("Hello");

Analogy

Jaise sentence me full stop missing ho.

2️⃣ Logical Bug

Code run karta hai but result wrong deta hai.

Example:

Wrong formula.

Analogy

Calculator sahi chal raha hai but formula galat hai.

3️⃣ Performance Bug

Software slow ho jata hai.

Example:

Website heavy traffic me slow ho jata hai.

Example platforms like Amazon kabhi kabhi sale ke time slow ho jata hai.

Analogy

Jaise highway pe traffic jam ho jaye.

Examples of Defects

1️⃣ Login failure
2️⃣ Form submission error
3️⃣ Website slow ho jana
4️⃣ Broken links

Analogy

Jaise lift ka button press karo aur lift aaye hi na.
